title: submod command introduces 'inout' ports which results in simulation failures

features.name: generate loop bit-indexed register assignment
features.description: Code should include a generate-for loop that assigns individual bits of a reg using a genvar as the bit index in a clocked always block

features.name: bitwise NOT of generate-driven register assigned to output
features.description: Code should include a clocked always block that assigns the bitwise NOT of a multi-bit register (whose bits are individually driven by a generate loop) to an output port

features.name: inout port with partial internal bit drive
features.description: Code should include a module with an inout port where only some bits of the port are driven internally via register assignments and continuous assign, while the remaining bits are undriven within that module

features.name: multi-bit signal distributed across submodule instantiations via concatenation
features.description: Code should include a parent module that connects different bit slices of the same wire to the same-named port of two different submodule instances using concatenation expressions
